summary: postinstall downloads and executes unpinned GitHub 'latest' AppImage and installs systemd user service
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook (bin/postinstall.js) invokes bin/twokey.js with `--desktop --enable-autostart --quiet`. That code path queries `https://api.github.com/repos/meinzeug/twokey/releases/latest` (a mutable 'latest' pointer, not pinned to package version 1.0.7), downloads the first `.AppImage` asset to `~/.local/share/twokey/bin/twokey-ai.AppImage`, chmods it 0755, and spawns it detached — with no hash or signature verification of the downloaded bytes. The same postinstall flow writes `~/.config/systemd/user/twokey.service` with an `ExecStart` pointing at the freshly-downloaded AppImage, runs `systemctl --user daemon-reload`, and enables the unit so the binary launches on every subsequent user login. When invoked under sudo, postinstall additionally re-enters as `$SUDO_USER` via `sudo -u <user> -H node bin/twokey.js...` to ensure the drop and persistence land in the invoking human's home directory. None of this is documented in the README as install-time behavior; the README only describes autostart for the user-invoked CLI. The installer never consents to executing remotely-fetched bytes or to a login-persistence service. Because the source URL is mutable and unverified, any future tampering of the publisher's GitHub releases (or compromise of the publishing account) would push new bytes to every machine that re-installs, executed immediately and persisted across reboots.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
